response:
# Triage plan — `04-monorepo` hotspots

Two axes drive the order: **fan-in** (how many places break if you change the file's surface) and **internal complexity** (how much an in-file refactor pays off). Low fan-in + big function = cheap, high-value cleanup. High fan-in + utility surface = leave alone, change only additively.

## Refactor first

**1. `src/esbuild/index.ts` — `runEsbuild` (lines 61–297, 237 lines)**
Fan-in **1**, fan-out 11. Almost nothing imports this file, so extracting helpers inside `runEsbuild` won't ripple — the high fan-out just means the function pulls in many deps, not that callers depend on its shape. `runEsbuild` is 4.0× the domain threshold and also flagged as an Option Bag Junk Drawer (38 distinct `options` reads). Split into named sub-steps + a typed options shape; this is the highest payoff / lowest risk move in the repo.

**2. `src/cli-main.ts` — `main` (lines 11–163, 153 lines)**
Blast radius **0.04** (CLI entry, nothing imports it). 2.5× threshold and another Option Bag offender. Extract argv parsing, config resolution, and the run dispatch into named functions. Zero ripple risk.

**3. `src/esbuild/postcss.ts` — `postcssPlugin` / `setup` (140 / 126 lines)**
Two nested god functions in a plugin file. Plugin files have small surfaces (returned plugin object), so internal extraction is contained. Also has Return Shape Roulette (3 conflicting object shapes with 0% key overlap) — fix the return types while you're in there.

## Refactor carefully (load-bearing but worth it)

**4. `src/index.ts` — `build` (lines 167–459, 293 lines) and `mainTasks` (194 lines)**
Fan-in **4**, fan-out **17**. The file is 459 lines with 28 top-level functions. The four importers care about the exported `build` *signature*, not its internals — so **extract helpers inside the file first** (cheap, no ripple), and only consider splitting the file as a follow-up. Also flagged False Identity (`build` does side effects despite the pure-sounding name): rename or extract the side-effecting parts as part of the same pass. Don't change the export shape in this PR.

## Leave alone (high fan-in — additive changes only)

**5. `src/utils.ts` — fan-in 18, 424 lines, 33 top-level functions**
Eighteen files import this. The God File finding is real but **splitting it is a separate, scoped project** — any rename or move breaks 18 importers. Treat as load-bearing. If individual helpers need work, edit them in place; don't restructure the module.

**6. `src/plugin.ts` — fan-in 10, `buildFinished` (103 lines)**
Ten importers and blast_radius 0.58. The 103-line `buildFinished` is tempting but the agent_risk score (0.66) is driven by the connectivity, not the size. Refactor only if there's a concrete bug to fix; otherwise the cost of breaking 10 callers outweighs the cleanup win.

**7. `src/log.ts` — fan-in 9, fan-out 0**
Pure logging helper, 9 importers. Don't touch the signatures; logging breakage is silent and ripples everywhere.

## Don't bother

**`src/rollup.ts` (fan-in 0, fan-out 7)** and **`src/api-extractor.ts` (fan-in 1, fan-out 6)** — both barely cross the p95 fan-out cutoff. Their large functions (`getRollupConfig` 122 lines, `rollupDtsFiles` 85 lines) are worth a cleanup *if you're already editing them*, but neither earns dedicated work — low connectivity, low blast radius, no current bug signal. **`test/utils.ts`** is test infrastructure; the fan-in 9 is expected.

## Summary order

| # | File | Why first/last |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `src/esbuild/index.ts` | fan-in 1 → free to restructure; biggest function in the repo |
| 2 | `src/cli-main.ts` | blast_radius 0.04; pure entry-point cleanup |
| 3 | `src/esbuild/postcss.ts` | self-contained plugin; fix return shapes at the same time |
| 4 | `src/index.ts` (internal only) | extract within the file; do **not** change exports |
| — | `src/utils.ts`, `src/plugin.ts`, `src/log.ts` | fan-in 9–18; additive changes only |
| — | `src/rollup.ts`, `src/api-extractor.ts` | borderline hotspots, no current pain |
